item.title: A security lapse at prison payphone service Pay Tel publicly exposed over 300K callers’ driver’s licenses
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Pay Tel secured the publicly exposed data after security researchers discovered the leak containing callers' sensitive ID documents and inmate communications.

item.title: Why Paris may be the most important AI city outside Silicon Valley
item.dc:creator: Chris Pullam
item.description: Europe’s startup ecosystem has matured significantly; its founders are increasingly willing to scale companies domestically instead of immediately looking to relocate to the U.S.

item.title: In just 3 weeks, StrictlyVC is coming to Los Angeles
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: StrictlyVC Los Angeles is on June 18. Join for meaningful networking and fireside chats with leaders from Mach Industries, Shinkei Systems, and more. Register today.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline extended to June 8 after overwhelming demand
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: After overwhelming demand from founders around the world, TechCrunch has extended the Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline to June 8. Nominate a standout startup or apply yours today.

item.title: Corgi announces $106M raise at $2.6B valuation — double what it was worth three weeks ago
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Insurance tech Corgi announced today an $106 million Series B1 raise, valuing the company at $2.6 billion, just three weeks after announcing a $160 million Series B.

item.title: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new ‘dynamic workflow’ tool
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: The new Opus model comes with a tool called Dynamic Workflows, for coordinating swarms of subagents.

item.title: Waymo’s newest robotaxi is Chinese-made, built to make money, and now accepting riders
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The launch of the Ojai minivan robotaxi comes after years of development and testing, but arrives amid a challenging time for Waymo.

item.title: U.S. says troops were targeted with location data, as senator warns ad industry is a ‘national security threat’
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: One leading privacy lawmaker said it was time to "start treating the adtech industry as a national security threat."

item.title: How long is Anthropic’s lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary.
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Elon Musk is publicly reframing xAI’s massive Anthropic compute deal as short-term and cancellable, despite SpaceX’s own S-1 filing describing payments through May 2029.

item.title: Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Sesame’s new iOS app brings its conversational AI agents to the public, offering more natural back-and-forth interactions designed to feel less like traditional chatbots and more like talking to a person.

item.title: Slate Auto will start taking orders for its low-cost EV on June 24
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Bezos-backed EV startup has yet to announce final pricing for its vehicle, which is supposed to start shipping by the end of this year.

item.title: Sneak peek at new Siri app reveals Apple’s plans to take on ChatGPT and more
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: New renders offer a closer look at Apple’s planned AI overhaul for iOS 27, including a redesigned Siri experience powered and standalone Siri app.

item.title: Rivian under investigation over rear suspension failures on R1 models
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: At least two owners have lost control of their vehicles after a critical suspension component broke. In both cases, the vehicles had been previously serviced.

item.title: RSI is the new AGI — and it’s just as hard to pin down
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: A new crop of AI labs are focused on recursive self-improvement — but the goal is proving elusive.

item.title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Databricks’ co-founder on what kills enterprise AI deals
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Enterprise AI is entering a different phase now, one where enterprises are no longer evaluating whether AI is exciting. They are evaluating whether it is safe to deploy broadly.

item.title: YouTube adds new podcast features, including an AI recommendation tool and ‘Auto speed’
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The update signals YouTube's ongoing efforts to compete with other platforms for podcast audiences.

item.title: 2 days left: Lock in ticket savings of up to $410 to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Savings of up to $410 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets end tomorrow, May 29, 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now to save and join 10,000+ tech leaders on October 13-15 in San Francisco.

item.title: Visa invests in Replit to power agentic payments for developers
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Visa said that over 1,000 employees have been using Replit for prototyping and development.

item.title: Has the hunt for AI compute uncovered the next Cerebras?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: General Compute is betting SambaNova will be the next breakout chipmaker.

item.title: Oura unveils its Ring 5 with a thinner, lighter design starting at $399
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The ring is 40% smaller than its predecessor, and comes with more accurate sensing, enhanced battery life, and more.
